preview_text:
The model forecasts a heavily one-sided contest, with Switzerland favored at 81% to win. Qatar's probability of victory stands at just 3%, leaving the underdog little realistic pathway to three points on a neutral pitch.

The win-draw-loss distribution reflects a large competitive gap. While a draw registers as plausible at 16%, the model assigns Switzerland a decisive edge over both alternative outcomes combined. The match structure points toward Swiss dominance, though the draw probability suggests the result is not a foregone conclusion.

The model and sportsbook consensus are broadly aligned. Switzerland's win probability differs by just 3 percentage points (81% vs. 78%), and the draw estimate is nearly identical. Polymarket prices sit between the two, reinforcing convergence across all three sources on the underlying match likelihood. The dashboard presents their agreement without claiming superiority for any single forecast method.
